title: Hemionitis subvillosa
taxonomy: Hemionitis subvillosa is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger group of plants known as ferns, which are characterized by their vascular tissue and ability to reproduce via spores. The genus Hemionitis is a relatively small group of ferns, with only a few species found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Hemionitis subvillosa is native to the tropical regions of Asia, and is often found growing in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Hemionitis subvillosa is further divided into various taxonomic ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.

intro: Hemionitis subvillosa, also known as the hairy hemionitis, is a species of fern native to Asia and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its hairy rhizome and subdeltoid leaflets. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis subvillosa include flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly processed into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in formulations for stress relief, skin care, and digestive support.
